---
title: "Network Embedding"
subtitle: "Lecture 08 · Advanced Topics in Network Science"
author: "Sadamori Kojaku"
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: dark
    width: 1280
    height: 720
    slide-number: true
    highlight-style: github-dark
    fig-align: center
    margin: 0.06
    transition: slide
    smaller: true
jupyter: advnetsci
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
---

```{=html}
<style>
/* ── Typography ─────────────────────────────────────── */
.reveal .slides section {
  font-size: 0.82em;
  line-height: 1.45;
}
.reveal h1 { font-size: 1.7em; margin-bottom: 0.3em; }
.reveal h2 { font-size: 1.25em; margin-bottom: 0.25em; }
.reveal h3 { font-size: 1.05em; margin-bottom: 0.15em; }

/* ── Math overflow ─────────────────────────────────── */
.katex-display { overflow-x: auto; overflow-y: hidden; padding: 0.1em 0; }
.katex-display .katex { font-size: 0.88em !important; }
.katex { font-size: 0.9em !important; }

/* ── Code blocks ───────────────────────────────────── */
.reveal pre code { font-size: 0.75em; max-height: 320px; }
.reveal pre { margin: 0.3em 0; }

/* ── Callouts ──────────────────────────────────────── */
.callout { font-size: 0.88em; margin: 0.4em 0; }

/* ── OJS interactive slides ────────────────────────── */
.ojs-viz {
  background: #161b2e;
  border-radius: 8px;
  padding: 8px;
  display: inline-block;
}
.viz-row { display: flex; gap: 12px; align-items: flex-start; justify-content: center; }
.viz-controls { display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 6px; min-width: 160px; }
label { color: #ccc; font-size: 0.8em; }
input[type=range] { width: 140px; accent-color: #4ecdc4; }

/* ── Columns spacing ───────────────────────────────── */
.reveal .columns { gap: 1.5em; }
</style>
```

```{python}
#| label: py-setup
import numpy as np
import networkx as nx
from sklearn.cluster import KMeans

# ── Karate club ────────────────────────────────────────
G   = nx.karate_club_graph()
A   = nx.to_numpy_array(G)
n   = int(A.shape[0])

# ── Adjacency eigen (descending) ──────────────────────
ev_a, U_a = np.linalg.eigh(A)
idx  = np.argsort(ev_a)[::-1]
ev_a = ev_a[idx];  U_a = U_a[:, idx]

# ── Reconstruction errors d = 1..20 ───────────────────
def recon_err(d):
    Ar = sum(float(ev_a[k]) * np.outer(U_a[:,k], U_a[:,k]) for k in range(d))
    return float(0.5 * np.linalg.norm(A - Ar, "fro")**2)

recon_errors = [recon_err(d) for d in range(1, 21)]

# ── Laplacian eigen (ascending) ───────────────────────
D_mat = np.diag(A.sum(1))
L     = D_mat - A
ev_l, U_l = np.linalg.eigh(L)

# ── Spring layout (fixed seed) ────────────────────────
pos   = nx.spring_layout(G, seed=42)
npos  = [[float(pos[i][0]), float(pos[i][1])] for i in range(n)]

# ── Graph topology ────────────────────────────────────
edges_js = [{"s": int(u), "t": int(v)} for u, v in G.edges()]
faction  = [0 if G.nodes[i]["club"] == "Mr. Hi" else 1 for i in range(n)]

ojs_define(
    A_mat        = A.tolist(),
    n_nodes      = n,
    ev_adj       = ev_a.tolist(),
    U_adj        = U_a.tolist(),
    recon_errors = recon_errors,
    ev_lap       = ev_l.tolist(),
    U_lap        = U_l.tolist(),
    npos         = npos,
    edges_js     = edges_js,
    faction      = faction,
)
```

<!-- ════════════════════════════════════════════════════ -->
<!-- TITLE                                               -->
<!-- ════════════════════════════════════════════════════ -->

## Network Embedding 🌐 {.center}

**Task**: Map each node of a graph into a low-dimensional vector such that:

1. The embedding can *reconstruct* the network with high accuracy.
2. The embedding should be *low-dimensional* ($d \ll N$).

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="55%"}
Formally, we want $\mathbf{U} \in \mathbb{R}^{N \times d}$ where each row $\mathbf{u}_i$ is the embedding of node $i$, and nodes with similar roles in the network end up close in $\mathbb{R}^d$.

**Why does this matter?** Standard ML algorithms work on vectors. Embedding lets us apply them to graphs.
:::
::: {.column width="45%"}
$$
\underbrace{\mathbf{A}}_{\text{graph}} \;\longrightarrow\; \underbrace{\mathbf{U} \in \mathbb{R}^{N \times d}}_{\text{embedding}}
$$

**Q**: How would you go about finding such $\mathbf{U}$?
:::
:::

---

## Idea 1: Network Reconstruction

Compress adjacency matrix $\mathbf{A}$ into $\mathbf{U} \in \mathbb{R}^{N \times d}$ by minimizing the **reconstruction error**:

$$
\min_{\mathbf{U}} \;\frac{1}{2}\|\mathbf{A} - \mathbf{U}\mathbf{U}^\top\|_F^2
$$

with orthonormal columns: $\mathbf{u}_i^\top \mathbf{u}_j = 0 \;(\forall i \neq j)$ and $\|\mathbf{u}_k\|^2 = 1$.

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
For the 1-d case ($\mathbf{U} = \lambda^{1/2}\mathbf{u}$), this becomes:

$$\min_{\mathbf{u},\lambda} \; \frac{1}{2}\|\mathbf{A} - \lambda\,\mathbf{u}\mathbf{u}^\top\|_F^2$$

Setting the gradient $\partial/\partial u_i = 0$ gives...
:::
::: {.column width="50%"}
**Q**: Try solving this. Expand the Frobenius norm and differentiate w.r.t. $u_i$.

*Hint*: Treat $\lambda$ and all $u_j$ ($j \neq i$) as constants.
:::
:::

---

## Proof: Gradient → Eigenvalue Problem {.scrollable}

Expanding the objective and differentiating w.r.t. $u_i$:

$$
\frac{\partial}{\partial u_i}\left[\frac{1}{2}\sum_{i,j}(A_{ij} - \lambda u_i u_j)^2\right]
= -2\lambda\left[\sum_{j \neq i} A_{ij}u_j - \lambda u_i \sum_{j \neq i}u_j^2\right] - 2\lambda A_{ii}u_i + 2\lambda^2 u_i^3 = 0
$$

Collecting terms (using $\sum_j u_j^2 = 1$):

$$
\sum_{j=1}^N A_{ij}\,u_j \;=\; \lambda\,u_i \quad\Longrightarrow\quad \boxed{\mathbf{A}\mathbf{u} = \lambda\mathbf{u}}
$$

This is the **eigenvalue problem** of $\mathbf{A}$. There are $N$ solutions $(\\lambda_k, \mathbf{u}_k)$. Which eigenvector gives the smallest reconstruction error?

---

## Which Eigenvector to Choose?

Expand the Frobenius norm using $\|\mathbf{M}\|_F^2 = \text{Tr}(\mathbf{M}^\top\mathbf{M})$:

$$
\tfrac{1}{2}\|\mathbf{A} - \lambda\mathbf{u}\mathbf{u}^\top\|_F^2
= \underbrace{\tfrac{1}{2}\text{Tr}(\mathbf{A}^\top\mathbf{A})}_{\text{const.}}
- \lambda\underbrace{\text{Tr}(\mathbf{u}^\top\mathbf{A}\mathbf{u})}_{=\,\lambda}
+ \tfrac{\lambda^2}{2}\underbrace{\text{Tr}(\mathbf{u}^\top\mathbf{u}\mathbf{u}^\top\mathbf{u})}_{=\,1}
= -\lambda^2 + \text{const.}
$$

The error is $-\lambda^2 + C$, which is **minimized when $\lambda$ is the largest eigenvalue**.

**For $d$ dimensions**: the optimal $\mathbf{U}$ is formed from the $d$ eigenvectors of $\mathbf{A}$ corresponding to the **top-$d$ eigenvalues**.

---

## 🔬 Interactive: Spectral Reconstruction

```{ojs}
//| label: ojs-recon-setup
d3   = require("d3@7")
N    = n_nodes

// ── helpers ───────────────────────────────────────────────
function buildRecon(d) {
  const Ar = Array.from({length: N}, () => new Float64Array(N));
  for (let k = 0; k < d; k++) {
    const lam = ev_adj[k];
    for (let i = 0; i < N; i++)
      for (let j = 0; j < N; j++)
        Ar[i][j] += lam * U_adj[i][k] * U_adj[j][k];
  }
  return Ar;
}

function heatSVG(mat, {title="", sz=6, div=true, clamp=[-1,1]} = {}) {
  const n  = mat.length;
  const W  = n * sz, H = n * sz;
  const cs = div
    ? d3.scaleDiverging(t => d3.interpolateRdBu(1-t)).domain([clamp[0], 0, clamp[1]])
    : d3.scaleSequential(d3.interpolateBlues).domain([0, 1]);
  const svg = d3.create("svg").attr("width", W).attr("height", H + 18)
    .style("border-radius","4px").style("background","#0d1117");
  if (title)
    svg.append("text").attr("x", W/2).attr("y", 13)
      .attr("text-anchor","middle").attr("fill","#aaa")
      .attr("font-size","11px").text(title);
  const g = svg.append("g").attr("transform","translate(0,18)");
  for (let i = 0; i < n; i++)
    for (let j = 0; j < n; j++)
      g.append("rect").attr("x", j*sz).attr("y", i*sz)
        .attr("width", sz).attr("height", sz)
        .attr("fill", cs(mat instanceof Float64Array ? mat[i*n+j] : mat[i][j]));
  return svg.node();
}

function curveSVG(errors, currentD, {W=460, H=150} = {}) {
  const m = {top:10, right:20, bottom:32, left:50};
  const iw = W - m.left - m.right, ih = H - m.top - m.bottom;
  const x  = d3.scaleLinear().domain([1, errors.length]).range([0, iw]);
  const y  = d3.scaleLinear().domain([0, d3.max(errors)]).range([ih, 0]);
  const svg = d3.create("svg").attr("width", W).attr("height", H)
    .style("background","#0d1117").style("border-radius","4px");
  const g = svg.append("g").attr("transform",`translate(${m.left},${m.top})`);
  // line
  g.append("path").datum(errors)
    .attr("fill","none").attr("stroke","#4ecdc4").attr("stroke-width",2)
    .attr("d", d3.line().x((_,i)=>x(i+1)).y(d=>y(d)));
  // dots
  g.selectAll("circle").data(errors).join("circle")
    .attr("cx",(_,i)=>x(i+1)).attr("cy",d=>y(d)).attr("r",3)
    .attr("fill", (_,i) => i+1 === currentD ? "#ff6b6b" : "#4ecdc4");
  // current vertical line
  g.append("line")
    .attr("x1", x(currentD)).attr("x2", x(currentD))
    .attr("y1", 0).attr("y2", ih)
    .attr("stroke","#ff6b6b").attr("stroke-dasharray","4,3").attr("stroke-width",1.5);
  // axes
  g.append("g").attr("transform",`translate(0,${ih})`)
    .call(d3.axisBottom(x).ticks(10).tickFormat(d3.format("d")))
    .selectAll("text,line,path").style("stroke","#666").style("fill","#888").style("font-size","10px");
  g.append("g").call(d3.axisLeft(y).ticks(4))
    .selectAll("text,line,path").style("stroke","#666").style("fill","#888").style("font-size","10px");
  g.append("text").attr("x", iw/2).attr("y", ih+28)
    .attr("text-anchor","middle").attr("fill","#888").attr("font-size","11px").text("d");
  g.append("text").attr("transform","rotate(-90)").attr("x",-ih/2).attr("y",-40)
    .attr("text-anchor","middle").attr("fill","#888").attr("font-size","11px").text("Reconstruction error");
  return svg.node();
}
```

```{ojs}
//| label: ojs-recon-vis
viewof d_val = Inputs.range([1, 20], {value: 5, step: 1, label: "d (eigenvectors)"})

Ar = buildRecon(d_val)
residual = Array.from({length: N}, (_,i) => Array.from({length: N}, (_,j) => A_mat[i][j] - Ar[i][j]))
maxAr    = d3.max(Array.from({length:N*N}, (_,k) => Math.abs(Ar[Math.floor(k/N)][k%N])))
curErr   = recon_errors[d_val - 1]

htA   = heatSVG(A_mat,   {title:"Original A",         sz:6, div:false})
htAr  = heatSVG(Ar,      {title:`Reconstructed (d=${d_val})`, sz:6, div:true, clamp:[-maxAr, maxAr]})
htRes = heatSVG(residual,{title:`Residual (err=${curErr.toFixed(0)})`, sz:6, div:true, clamp:[-0.6,0.6]})
cv    = curveSVG(recon_errors, d_val)

html`<div style="display:flex;gap:10px;align-items:flex-start;justify-content:center;flex-wrap:wrap">
  ${htA}${htAr}${htRes}
  <div>${cv}</div>
</div>`
```

---

## Idea 2: Graph Cut ✂️

**Graph Cut Problem**: Partition nodes into two disjoint sets $Q$ and $S$ by cutting the fewest edges:

$$
\min_{Q,S} \;\text{cut}(Q,S) = \sum_{i \in Q}\sum_{j \in S} A_{ij}
$$

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="60%"}
Minimizing raw cut is bad — it tends to isolate a single node. We need a **balanced** cut.

**Ratio Cut** penalizes imbalanced partitions:

$$
\text{RatioCut}(Q,S) = \sum_{i \in Q}\sum_{j \in S} A_{ij}\left(\frac{1}{|Q|}+\frac{1}{|S|}\right)
$$
:::
::: {.column width="40%"}
**Normalized Cut** uses volume instead of size:

$$
\text{NC}(Q,S) = \frac{\text{cut}(Q,S)}{\text{vol}(Q)} + \frac{\text{cut}(Q,S)}{\text{vol}(S)}
$$

where $\text{vol}(Q) = \sum_{i \in Q} k_i$.
:::
:::

---

## Ratio Cut → Laplacian Form {.scrollable}

Define the indicator vector:
$$
x_i = \begin{cases}\sqrt{|S|/|Q|} & i \in Q \\ -\sqrt{|Q|/|S|} & i \in S\end{cases}
\qquad\Rightarrow\qquad \sum_i x_i = 0,\quad \sum_i x_i^2 = N
$$

Note $(x_i - x_j)^2 = 0$ when $i,j$ are in the same partition, and $(x_i-x_j)^2 = N^2/(|Q||S|)$ otherwise. Then:

$$
\begin{aligned}
\text{RatioCut}(Q,S)
&= \frac{1}{2N}\sum_{i,j}A_{ij}(x_i-x_j)^2
= \frac{1}{N}\sum_{i,j}L_{ij}\,x_i x_j
= \frac{1}{N}\,\mathbf{x}^\top\mathbf{L}\mathbf{x}
\end{aligned}
$$

where the **combinatorial Laplacian** is $\mathbf{L} = \mathbf{D} - \mathbf{A}$.

So minimizing RatioCut is equivalent to:

$$
\min_{\mathbf{x}}\;\mathbf{x}^\top\mathbf{L}\mathbf{x}
\quad\text{s.t.}\quad \mathbf{x}^\top\mathbf{1}=0,\;\mathbf{x}^\top\mathbf{x}=N,\;x_i\in\left\{\sqrt{|S|/|Q|},-\sqrt{|Q|/|S|}\right\}
$$

This is **NP-hard**. Let's relax the discrete constraint.

---

## Spectral Relaxation

Relax $x_i \in \mathbb{R}$ with normalization $\mathbf{x}^\top\mathbf{x}=1$ and $\mathbf{x}^\top\mathbf{1}=0$:

$$\min_{\mathbf{x}}\;\mathbf{x}^\top\mathbf{L}\mathbf{x} \quad\text{s.t.}\quad \mathbf{x}^\top\mathbf{x}=1,\;\mathbf{x}\perp\mathbf{1}$$

From the eigenvalue equation $\mathbf{L}\mathbf{x}=\lambda\mathbf{x}$:

$$\mathbf{x}^\top\mathbf{L}\mathbf{x} = \lambda\,\mathbf{x}^\top\mathbf{x} = \lambda$$

So the minimum is achieved at the **smallest eigenvalue**... but wait:

::: {.callout-warning}
The smallest eigenvalue $\lambda_1 = 0$ of $\mathbf{L}$ has eigenvector $\mathbf{x}_1 \propto \mathbf{1}$, which violates $\mathbf{x}\perp\mathbf{1}$.
The **second smallest** eigenvector $\mathbf{x}_2$ (the **Fiedler vector**) is the true solution.
For $k$-way partitioning, use eigenvectors $\mathbf{x}_2, \ldots, \mathbf{x}_{k}$.
:::

---

## 🔬 Interactive: Spectral Clustering

```{ojs}
//| label: ojs-clust-setup
function graphSVG(positions, edges, colors, walkPath, {W=420, H=380, rNode=5} = {}) {
  // Scale positions from [-1,1] to SVG coords
  const xs = positions.map(p=>p[0]), ys = positions.map(p=>p[1]);
  const xExt = d3.extent(xs), yExt = d3.extent(ys);
  const pad = 30;
  const scX = d3.scaleLinear().domain(xExt).range([pad, W-pad]);
  const scY = d3.scaleLinear().domain(yExt).range([H-pad, pad]);

  const svg = d3.create("svg").attr("width",W).attr("height",H)
    .style("background","#0d1117").style("border-radius","6px");

  // Edges
  svg.selectAll("line").data(edges).join("line")
    .attr("x1",d=>scX(positions[d.s][0])).attr("y1",d=>scY(positions[d.s][1]))
    .attr("x2",d=>scX(positions[d.t][0])).attr("y2",d=>scY(positions[d.t][1]))
    .attr("stroke",d => {
      if (!walkPath) return "#444";
      const inWalk = walkPath.some(([a,b])=>(a===d.s&&b===d.t)||(a===d.t&&b===d.s));
      return inWalk ? "#ff6b6b" : "#333";
    })
    .attr("stroke-width", d => {
      if (!walkPath) return 0.8;
      const inWalk = walkPath.some(([a,b])=>(a===d.s&&b===d.t)||(a===d.t&&b===d.s));
      return inWalk ? 2.5 : 0.6;
    })
    .attr("opacity", d => {
      if (!walkPath) return 0.6;
      const inWalk = walkPath.some(([a,b])=>(a===d.s&&b===d.t)||(a===d.t&&b===d.s));
      return inWalk ? 1 : 0.25;
    });

  // Nodes
  svg.selectAll("circle").data(colors).join("circle")
    .attr("cx", (_,i)=>scX(positions[i][0]))
    .attr("cy", (_,i)=>scY(positions[i][1]))
    .attr("r", (_,i) => {
      if (!walkPath) return rNode;
      const inWalk = walkPath.flat().includes(i);
      return inWalk ? rNode + 2 : rNode;
    })
    .attr("fill", d => d)
    .attr("stroke", (_,i) => {
      if (!walkPath) return "#222";
      if (walkPath.length && walkPath[0][0]===i) return "#fff";
      if (walkPath.length && walkPath[walkPath.length-1][1]===i) return "#ffd700";
      return "#222";
    })
    .attr("stroke-width", (_,i) => {
      if (!walkPath) return 0.8;
      if (walkPath.length && (walkPath[0][0]===i || walkPath[walkPath.length-1][1]===i)) return 2;
      return 0.5;
    });

  // Node labels (small)
  svg.selectAll("text").data(colors).join("text")
    .attr("x", (_,i)=>scX(positions[i][0]))
    .attr("y", (_,i)=>scY(positions[i][1])+3.5)
    .attr("text-anchor","middle").attr("font-size","6.5px")
    .attr("fill","#fff").attr("pointer-events","none")
    .text((_,i)=>i);

  return svg.node();
}

function scatterSVG(xs, ys, colors, {W=330, H=310, xlabel="x₂", ylabel="x₃"} = {}) {
  const m = {top:15, right:15, bottom:35, left:45};
  const iw = W-m.left-m.right, ih = H-m.top-m.bottom;
  const sx = d3.scaleLinear().domain(d3.extent(xs)).nice().range([0, iw]);
  const sy = d3.scaleLinear().domain(d3.extent(ys)).nice().range([ih, 0]);
  const svg = d3.create("svg").attr("width",W).attr("height",H)
    .style("background","#0d1117").style("border-radius","6px");
  const g = svg.append("g").attr("transform",`translate(${m.left},${m.top})`);
  // grid
  g.selectAll(".gx").data(sx.ticks(5)).join("line")
    .attr("x1",d=>sx(d)).attr("x2",d=>sx(d)).attr("y1",0).attr("y2",ih)
    .attr("stroke","#1e2535").attr("stroke-width",1);
  g.selectAll(".gy").data(sy.ticks(5)).join("line")
    .attr("x1",0).attr("x2",iw).attr("y1",d=>sy(d)).attr("y2",d=>sy(d))
    .attr("stroke","#1e2535").attr("stroke-width",1);
  // points
  g.selectAll("circle").data(colors).join("circle")
    .attr("cx",(_,i)=>sx(xs[i])).attr("cy",(_,i)=>sy(ys[i]))
    .attr("r",4).attr("fill",d=>d).attr("opacity",0.85);
  // labels
  g.selectAll("text.lbl").data(colors).join("text").attr("class","lbl")
    .attr("x",(_,i)=>sx(xs[i])+5).attr("y",(_,i)=>sy(ys[i])+3)
    .attr("font-size","6px").attr("fill","#888").text((_,i)=>i);
  // axes
  g.append("g").attr("transform",`translate(0,${ih})`)
    .call(d3.axisBottom(sx).ticks(4))
    .selectAll("text,path,line").style("fill","#888").style("stroke","#555").style("font-size","10px");
  g.append("g").call(d3.axisLeft(sy).ticks(4))
    .selectAll("text,path,line").style("fill","#888").style("stroke","#555").style("font-size","10px");
  g.append("text").attr("x",iw/2).attr("y",ih+30)
    .attr("text-anchor","middle").attr("fill","#aaa").attr("font-size","11px").text(xlabel);
  g.append("text").attr("transform","rotate(-90)").attr("x",-ih/2).attr("y",-35)
    .attr("text-anchor","middle").attr("fill","#aaa").attr("font-size","11px").text(ylabel);
  return svg.node();
}
```

```{ojs}
//| label: ojs-clust-vis
viewof k_val = Inputs.range([2, 5], {value: 2, step: 1, label: "k (clusters)"})

TAB10 = ["#4c78a8","#f58518","#e45756","#72b7b2","#54a24b","#eeca3b","#b279a2","#ff9da6","#9d755d","#bab0ac"]

clust_labels = {
  // Use Fiedler eigenvectors (skip eigvec 0 = const)
  const vecs = Array.from({length: N}, (_,i) => Array.from({length: k_val-1}, (_,k) => U_lap[i][k+1]));
  if (k_val === 2) return vecs.map(v => v[0] >= 0 ? 0 : 1);
  // Simple k-means
  let cents = vecs.slice(0, k_val).map(v => [...v]);
  let labs   = new Array(N).fill(0);
  for (let iter = 0; iter < 80; iter++) {
    for (let i = 0; i < N; i++) {
      let best = 0, bestD = Infinity;
      for (let c = 0; c < k_val; c++) {
        const d2 = vecs[i].reduce((s,v,j)=>s+(v-cents[c][j])**2, 0);
        if (d2 < bestD) { bestD=d2; best=c; }
      }
      labs[i] = best;
    }
    for (let c = 0; c < k_val; c++) {
      const members = vecs.filter((_,i)=>labs[i]===c);
      if (members.length) cents[c] = cents[c].map((_,j)=>d3.mean(members, v=>v[j]));
    }
  }
  return labs;
}

node_colors_clust = clust_labels.map(c => TAB10[c])

g_clust  = graphSVG(npos, edges_js, node_colors_clust, null, {W:420, H:360})
scatter  = scatterSVG(
  Array.from({length:N},(_,i)=>U_lap[i][1]),
  Array.from({length:N},(_,i)=>k_val > 2 ? U_lap[i][2] : U_lap[i][1] * 0 + (Math.random()-0.5)*0.01),
  node_colors_clust,
  {W:330, H:360, xlabel:"Fiedler vector x₂", ylabel: k_val > 2 ? "x₃" : "(jitter)"}
)

html`<div style="display:flex;gap:16px;align-items:flex-start;justify-content:center">
  <div>
    <div style="color:#aaa;font-size:11px;text-align:center;margin-bottom:4px">Graph (Karate Club)</div>
    ${g_clust}
  </div>
  <div>
    <div style="color:#aaa;font-size:11px;text-align:center;margin-bottom:4px">Laplacian Eigenmap</div>
    ${scatter}
  </div>
</div>`
```

---

## Laplacian Eigenmap 🔄

A closely related embedding: position connected nodes **close together** in Euclidean space.

**Objective**:
$$
\min_{\mathbf{u}}\;\sum_{i,j}A_{ij}\|\mathbf{u}_i - \mathbf{u}_j\|^2
= \min_{\mathbf{U}}\;\text{tr}(\mathbf{U}^\top\mathbf{L}\mathbf{U})
\quad\text{s.t.}\quad \mathbf{U}^\top\mathbf{D}\mathbf{U} = \mathbf{I}
$$

**Solution**: The columns of $\mathbf{U}$ are the eigenvectors $\mathbf{x}_2, \ldots, \mathbf{x}_{d+1}$ of the generalized eigenvalue problem:

$$\mathbf{L}\mathbf{x} = \lambda\,\mathbf{D}\mathbf{x}$$

The **interactive spectral clustering** demo above shows these eigenvectors directly. The Laplacian eigenmap is exactly the coordinate system where graph structure becomes geometry.

---

## Neural Embedding: The Key Idea

Spectral methods require eigendecomposition ($\mathcal{O}(N^3)$). For large networks, we need something faster.

**Insight from NLP**: *word2vec* learns word vectors by predicting which words appear near each other in sentences. If two words appear in similar contexts, they get similar vectors.

**The translation to graphs**:

1. Run **random walks** on the graph → sequences of nodes ("sentences")
2. Treat node sequences like word sequences
3. Train **word2vec (skip-gram)** to predict which nodes co-occur in walks

$$\mathcal{L} = \sum_t \sum_{0 < |j| \le c} \log P(v_{t+j} \mid v_t), \qquad P(v_O \mid v_I) = \frac{\exp(\mathbf{u}_{v_O}^\top \mathbf{u}_{v_I})}{\sum_v \exp(\mathbf{u}_v^\top \mathbf{u}_{v_I})}$$

**DeepWalk** (Perozzi et al., 2014) does exactly this with uniform random walks.

---

## node2vec: Biased Random Walks

**node2vec** (Grover & Leskovec, 2016) extends DeepWalk with a *biased* walk controlled by two parameters:

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="55%"}
$$
P(\text{next}=x \mid v, t) \propto
\begin{cases}
1/p & d(t,x) = 0 \text{ (backtrack to } t)\\
1   & d(t,x) = 1 \text{ (shared neighbor)}\\
1/q & d(t,x) = 2 \text{ (explore further)}
\end{cases}
$$

- **Low $p$**: frequent backtracking → **BFS-like**, explores local neighborhood → captures *structural equivalence*
- **Low $q$**: frequent exploration → **DFS-like**, ventures far → captures *community membership*
:::
::: {.column width="45%"}
The walk at node $v$ (came from $t$) assigns unnormalized weights to neighbors $x$:

```
x == t      →  weight 1/p
x ~ t       →  weight 1
otherwise   →  weight 1/q
```

Then normalize and sample. The walk is **second-order Markov** (depends on both current and previous node).
:::
:::

---

## 🔬 Interactive: node2vec Random Walk

```{ojs}
//| label: ojs-n2v
viewof rw_start = Inputs.range([0, N-1], {value: 0, step: 1, label: "Start node"})
viewof rw_steps = Inputs.range([3, 30],  {value: 12, step: 1, label: "Steps"})
viewof rw_p     = Inputs.range([0.1, 4], {value: 1.0, step: 0.1, label: "p (return)"})
viewof rw_q     = Inputs.range([0.1, 4], {value: 1.0, step: 0.1, label: "q (explore)"})

// ── Build adjacency list ───────────────────────────────
adj_list = {
  const al = Array.from({length: N}, () => []);
  for (const e of edges_js) { al[e.s].push(e.t); al[e.t].push(e.s); }
  return al;
}

// ── Biased random walk ────────────────────────────────
walk = {
  const seed_val = rw_start * 31 + rw_steps;  // pseudo-seed for stability
  const rng = (() => { let s = seed_val; return () => { s = (s * 1664525 + 1013904223) & 0xffffffff; return (s >>> 0) / 4294967296; }; })();
  const w = [rw_start];
  let prev = null;
  for (let step = 0; step < rw_steps - 1; step++) {
    const cur   = w[w.length - 1];
    const nbrs  = adj_list[cur];
    if (!nbrs.length) break;
    if (prev === null) {
      w.push(nbrs[Math.floor(rng() * nbrs.length)]);
    } else {
      const wts = nbrs.map(x => {
        if (x === prev) return 1 / rw_p;
        if (A_mat[prev][x] > 0) return 1.0;
        return 1 / rw_q;
      });
      const total = wts.reduce((a,b)=>a+b, 0);
      let r = rng() * total, chosen = nbrs[nbrs.length-1];
      for (let i = 0; i < nbrs.length; i++) { r -= wts[i]; if (r <= 0) { chosen = nbrs[i]; break; } }
      w.push(chosen);
    }
    prev = cur;
  }
  return w;
}

walk_edges = Array.from({length: walk.length-1}, (_,i) => [walk[i], walk[i+1]])
walk_colors = faction.map((f,i) => {
  if (i === walk[0]) return "#ffffff";
  if (i === walk[walk.length-1]) return "#ffd700";
  if (walk.includes(i)) return "#ff8c00";
  return f === 0 ? "#4c78a8" : "#e45756";
})

g_walk = graphSVG(npos, edges_js, walk_colors, walk_edges, {W:520, H:400})

walk_str = walk.join(" → ")

html`<div style="display:flex;gap:20px;align-items:flex-start;justify-content:center">
  <div>
    ${g_walk}
    <div style="color:#888;font-size:10px;margin-top:4px;text-align:center">
      ⬜ start &nbsp; 🟡 end &nbsp; 🟠 visited &nbsp; 🔵/🔴 faction
    </div>
  </div>
  <div style="max-width:340px">
    <div style="color:#aaa;font-size:11px;margin-bottom:8px"><b>Walk sequence:</b></div>
    <div style="color:#4ecdc4;font-size:10px;word-break:break-all;line-height:1.6;background:#0d1117;padding:8px;border-radius:4px">${walk_str}</div>
    <div style="color:#888;font-size:10.5px;margin-top:10px;line-height:1.7">
      <b style="color:#aaa">p = ${rw_p.toFixed(1)}</b> — ${rw_p < 1 ? "Low p: frequent backtracking (BFS-like)" : rw_p > 2 ? "High p: rarely returns (DFS-like)" : "Balanced return"}<br>
      <b style="color:#aaa">q = ${rw_q.toFixed(1)}</b> — ${rw_q < 1 ? "Low q: stays local (BFS-like, structural roles)" : rw_q > 2 ? "High q: explores far (DFS-like, community)" : "Balanced exploration"}
    </div>
  </div>
</div>`
```

---

## Summary

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
**Spectral methods** — minimize reconstruction or cut objectives; solution = eigenvectors

| Method | Matrix | Eigenvectors |
|---|---|---|
| Spectral emb. | $\mathbf{A}$ | Top-$d$ (largest $\lambda$) |
| Ratio Cut | $\mathbf{L}$ | 2nd–$(k+1)$th smallest |
| Norm. Cut | $\mathbf{L}_n$ | 2nd–$(k+1)$th smallest |
| Lap. Eigenmap | $\mathbf{L}$ | Same as Ratio Cut |

Pros: principled, interpretable.
Cons: $\mathcal{O}(N^3)$ eigendecomposition.
:::
::: {.column width="50%"}
**Neural methods** — sample random walks, train word2vec

| Model | Walk | Captures |
|---|---|---|
| DeepWalk | Uniform | Community |
| node2vec (low $q$) | DFS-like | Community |
| node2vec (low $p$) | BFS-like | Structural roles |

Pros: scalable, flexible.
Cons: hyperparameter tuning, less interpretable.

Both families embed nodes into $\mathbb{R}^d$ — downstream tasks (clustering, classification, link prediction) then use standard ML on the vectors.
:::
:::
